name: Arabic
register: Reverent contemporary Christian Arabic in the wording family of the Van Dyck Bible (فان دايك، 1865) — the most-trusted and best-loved Arabic scripture across Protestant and Orthodox communities — warmed toward the register of كتاب الحياة (Book of Life / NAV) where Van Dyck reads archaic. The filial vision is anchored in living Arabic-Christian usage: the Lord's-Prayer address «أبانا الذي في السموات» and the Trinitarian name الآب, which every Arabic-speaking believer hears as their own Father, never as the generic الله.
quote_convention: Arabic guillemets «…» for direct speech, with nested speech in single ‹…›; text is right-to-left.
poetry_convention: Poetry (Psalms, Prophets, canticles) set as separate indented lines, one poetic stich per line with the parallel colon on the following indented line; hemistich breaks preserved, right-to-left.

glossary.note: Filial: built on أب 'father' + السماوي 'heavenly'; the warm personal Father, never الله.
The beloved Lord's-Prayer address «أبانا الذي في السموات»; nominative أبونا, vocative أبانا. NEVER revert to الله or الرب.
The living Arabic-Christian name for God the Father (الآب والابن والروح القدس); distinct from the generic الله.
Definite filial form: الآب + السماوي; matches Trinitarian usage, not الله.
Van Dyck retains the Aramaic أبا (Abba) and glosses it الآب (Mark 14:36; Rom 8:15; Gal 4:6) — the intimate cry kept intact.
REVERSION / CONTRAST term — the standard Arabic-Bible word for God-as-God. Use only where English keeps 'God'; never as a substitute for أبونا.

REVERSION / CONTRAST term — the covenant name (Yahweh) rendered الرب in Arabic Bibles. NB: unlike Telugu, Arabic uses the same word الرب for the title 'Lord' applied to Jesus; disambiguate by context. Never a substitute for أبونا.
Standard Van Dyck term.
Relational (of the Father), not روح الله 'Spirit of God' — carries the filial أب stem.
Standard messianic self-title.
The Son (Jesus); the Trinitarian الابن answering to الآب.
Christological title; the standard Van Dyck ابن الله is retained (Sonship declared toward God). Reviewer to confirm vs ابن الآب.
John 3:16 (Van Dyck: ابنه الوحيد). وحيد = only-begotten / one-of-a-kind.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially: the special divine-kingdom word ملكوت + أبينا, not ملكوت الله. Confirmed pattern (John 3:3,5).
Christian-Arabic form يسوع (not the Qur'anic عيسى).
Standard title (John 3:28).
Where English keeps 'Messiah', Van Dyck transliterates مسيّا and glosses it المسيح (John 1:41; 4:25) — kept distinct from the title المسيح.
Standard Arabic-Bible term (also 'Testament', العهد القديم/الجديد).
Renders Hebrew ḥesed (loyal covenant love). Reviewer to confirm vs traditional Van Dyck رحمة / إحسان.
Renders racham / splanchnizomai (gut-level tenderness); Van Dyck uses رأفات / تحنن. The رحم/womb-tenderness resonance is available via مراحم if reviewer prefers.
Van Dyck البر for dikaiosynē / ṣedeq.
John 3:16 (Van Dyck: الحياة الأبدية).
Van Dyck reads يُولَد من فوق 'born from above' (anōthen, John 3:3,7); noun form: الولادة الجديدة. Reviewer to confirm جديد vs من فوق.
